Theorem of impossibility (for the majority agreement problem)
When there is a single malicious process, any distributed algorithm cannot terminate.
In other words, even if all processes except one are behaving correctly and following the instructions specified by the algorithm, it may not be possible to achieve majority agreement. output:
The Fourier Transform.
The Fourier Transform is one of several methods to convert signals in time and space to frequencies. A signal can be transformed using the Fourier Transform, which involves the multiplication of an imaginary function by a complex number.
The Fourier Transform is used in many applications including digital filtering, spectral analysis, and statistical modeling. In general, it produces spectra that are similar to those found in classical spectroscopy (spectra where emission or absorption occurs).

The consistency property is used to ensure that changes made during a transaction remain valid even after other users make their own transactions.
